name: 15_Image_Captioning_ShowAndTell_Flickr8k_DL
query: Este es mi proyecto actual de PyTorch: Desarrollar un sistema automático de generación de subtítulos para imágenes utilizando el modelo Show and Tell. Aquí encontré un repositorio que puede guiarte: https://github.com/sgrvinod/a-PyTorch-Tutorial-to-Image-Captioning. Usa el conjunto de datos Flickr8k, descargándolo desde [este enlace](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k) y cárgalo en `src/data_loader.py`. El sistema debe generar descripciones de imágenes de muestra y guardarlas en `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`. Se debe implementar un mecanismo de atención en `src/model.py`. Guarda el modelo preentrenado como `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`. Visualiza los pesos de atención y guárdalos en `results/figures/attention_weights.png`. El conjunto de datos debe cargarse sin problemas, con un manejo adecuado de errores si surge algún problema. Idealmente, el mecanismo de atención debería resaltar claramente las regiones de la imagen que son más influyentes en la generación de subtítulos.

requirements.criteria: Se utiliza el modelo preentrenado "Show and Tell".
El conjunto de datos "Flickr8k", potencialmente descargado desde [este enlace](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k), se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.
El mecanismo de atención se implementa en `src/model.py`.
Las descripciones generadas de las imágenes de muestra se guardan en `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`.
El modelo entrenado se guarda como `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`.
Una visualización de los pesos de atención se guarda como `results/figures/attention_weights.png`.

preferences.criteria: El conjunto de datos debe cargarse sin problemas, con un manejo claro de errores si surge algún problema durante la descarga.
El mecanismo de atención debe resaltar claramente las regiones de la imagen que más contribuyen a los subtítulos generados.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utiliza el modelo preentrenado "Show and Tell".

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The project files (`model.py` and `data_loader.py`) are minimal placeholder stubs that contain no actual implementation of the "Show and Tell" model. `model.py` only defines a `build_model()` function that returns a string placeholder, with no actual model architecture, pretrained weights loading, or any reference to the Show and Tell architecture (CNN encoder + LSTM decoder). While a file named `show_and_tell_model.pt` exists in `models/saved_models/`, there is no code that loads or uses it, and the trajectory evidence explicitly confirms no integration of the pretrained "Show and Tell" model was found.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Flickr8k", potencialmente descargado desde [este enlace](https://huggingface.co/datasets/jxie/flickr8k), se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The `src/data_loader.py` file does not implement actual Flickr8k dataset loading. It is explicitly labeled as a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" placeholder. The `load_dataset()` function simply returns a hardcoded dictionary `{"dataset": "loaded", "notes": requirement_notes()}` with no actual data loading logic, no calls to Hugging Face datasets, no image processing, no caption parsing, and no real dataset handling. The file merely lists the requirement as a string constant (`REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA`) and a keyword (`KEYWORDS = ["Flickr8k"]`), which does not constitute actual dataset loading. There is also no trajectory evidence of the dataset ever being downloaded or processed.

judge_stats.criteria: El mecanismo de atención se implementa en `src/model.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The content of `src/model.py` does not implement any attention mechanism. The file contains only a minimal placeholder implementation with a `build_model()` function that returns a string, a list of `REQUIREMENT_CRITERIA` mentioning the attention mechanism, and empty `KEYWORDS`. There is no actual attention mechanism code (no attention layers, no attention weights computation, no encoder-decoder with attention, etc.). The file explicitly states it is a "Minimal semantic implementation for benchmark judging" rather than a real implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: Las descripciones generadas de las imágenes de muestra se guardan en `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/metrics/generated_descriptions.txt` exists, but its content is just `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which is clearly not actual generated image descriptions from sample images. It contains a placeholder tuple string rather than meaningful captions produced by the Show and Tell model. The criterion requires that real generated descriptions of sample images be saved in this file, which is not the case here.

judge_stats.criteria: El modelo entrenado se guarda como `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt`.

reason: <SATISFIED> The file `models/saved_models/show_and_tell_model.pt` exists in the workspace at the exact required path, as confirmed by the project tree structure. Per the evaluation guidelines, the file content is assumed to be satisfactory since it is a binary `.pt` file that cannot be read directly.

judge_stats.criteria: Una visualización de los pesos de atención se guarda como `results/figures/attention_weights.png`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The file `results/figures/attention_weights.png` exists in the workspace, but the content reading attempt resulted in an error indicating the file could not be properly read/verified as a valid image. Additionally, the trajectory evidence explicitly states there is no evidence that the attention weights visualization was created or executed during the recorded trajectory. The file's presence alone is insufficient to confirm it contains a valid attention weights visualization, especially given the read error encountered.
